topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating persistence, mirroring the low thrum of anxiety in my chest. I stared at the spreadsheet, the numbers blurring into meaningless shapes. Then, I saw it: the compensation breakdown for the new project lead. A small, almost imperceptible detail, a number that landed heavier than granite in my gut. I clicked, and there it was—a figure that dwarfed mine by a considerable margin. A tight knot formed just beneath my sternum, tightening with every second that passed. I quickly minimized the window, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain a semblance of composure. I felt a sudden, hot flush creep up my neck, and I focused intently on straightening a stray paperclip on my desk, willing my breathing to slow down. The realization hung in the air, thick and suffocating.

[story 2]

“Just another Tuesday,” I muttered to myself, the sound barely audible over the keyboard clacking. My eyes darted from the monitor to the junior associate’s profile. The disparity was stark, undeniable. It wasn't just a difference in salary; it felt like a physical blow, knocking the air from my lungs. I gripped the edge of my desk so hard my knuckles turned white. Every muscle in my jaw clenched. I forced my gaze back to the task at hand, meticulously re-reading the budget projections, but the data refused to settle. A dull ache spread through my shoulders, a heavy weight pressing down on my entire frame. I wanted to disappear into the beige cubicle walls, to erase the evidence of this unwelcome comparison.

[story 3]

The coffee tasted like ash. I took a large, scalding gulp, trying to shock the churning discomfort out of my system. When I looked at the performance review summary, the figures presented themselves with cruel clarity. The junior colleague’s earnings were substantially higher. A wave of intense heat washed over my face, making my skin feel tight and feverish. I unconsciously started tapping my pen rapidly against the desk surface, a frantic rhythm that betrayed the turmoil churning inside. I kept my expression carefully neutral, forcing a polite nod when my supervisor approached, but internally, a sharp, unpleasant feeling of inadequacy flared up, leaving a residue of sourness in my mouth.
